description_long: This is a medium-sized detached house with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in dark brown shingles. The exterior walls are clad in light-colored brick, with a stone or concrete foundation visible at the base. The house features a covered front porch with steps leading up to the main entry, supported by dark posts and a small overhang. Windows are symmetrically arranged, with two on each visible side and additional windows on the upper story, all framed in dark trim. A prominent chimney rises from one side of the house, and the rear section includes a partially enclosed porch or veranda. The overall design is simple yet functional, with a focus on practicality and modest decorative elements.
description: A light brick house with a steep gable roof, covered front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement. Features include a prominent chimney, dark trim, and a partially enclosed rear veranda.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with steps, covered porch, and two front windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the chimney, two side windows, and the extended roof over the rear veranda.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the secondary entry, additional porch area, and symmetrical window placement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two upper and two lower windows, and a clear view of the gable roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered front porch with steps, dark posts, small overhang, and a single main door
